title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: July 12, 2026 - Summer League Heats Up, Major Offseason Moves Official!

items.headline: Blockbuster Deals Official: Giannis, Brown, Leonard, Ball Headed to New Teams!
items.summary: The NBA's July moratorium has officially ended, making several seismic offseason trades a reality. Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis are now officially members of the Miami Heat, while Jaylen Brown has been traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers. The Toronto Raptors have acquired Kawhi Leonard, and LaMelo Ball has been moved from the Charlotte Hornets to the Minnesota Timberwolves in a four-team trade.

items.headline: Wembanyama Takes Discount, Lakers Land Kessler, LeBron's Future Looms
items.summary: In a move showcasing his commitment to team building, Spurs star Victor Wembanyama signed a five-year, $252 million extension, reportedly waiving a bonus structure that could have reached $303 million to provide San Antonio with greater financial flexibility. Meanwhile, the Los Angeles Lakers have made a significant splash, acquiring rim-protecting center Walker Kessler from the Utah Jazz in exchange for two unprotected first-round picks (2031, 2033) and first-round swap rights (2028, 2030), a move reportedly championed by Luka Doncic. All eyes remain on LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent who is taking his time to decide his next destination, with the Cavaliers, Heat, Warriors, 76ers, Timberwolves, and Nuggets all in the mix.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Shining Bright, Praised by Ja Morant
items.summary: Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija, already a 2026 NBA All-Star, is drawing significant praise from new teammate Ja Morant. Morant highlighted Avdija's competitive mindset during discussions at the NBA Summer League, noting his ability to frustrate opponents and keep teammates sharp.

items.headline: Deni Avdija: A 2026 All-Star with Competitive Edge
Hornets GM Explains LaMelo Ball Trade for Long-Term Vision
items.summary: Charlotte Hornets General Manager Jeff Peterson articulated the strategic thinking behind trading star guard LaMelo Ball to the Minnesota Timberwolves. Peterson emphasized that the move, which brought Naz Reid, significant draft capital, and a record $40.7 million trade exception, was made with the long-term success of the franchise in mind.

items.headline: Wizards Lock Up Trae Young, Add Khris Middleton
items.summary: The Washington Wizards have been busy in the offseason, officially re-signing star guard Trae Young to a new deal. Additionally, the Wizards acquired veteran forward Khris Middleton from the Dallas Mavericks in a sign-and-trade agreement, part of a massive six-team transaction.

items.headline: Raptors Facing Cap Challenges Post-Leonard Trade
items.summary: Following the acquisition of Kawhi Leonard, the Toronto Raptors are reportedly navigating significant salary cap challenges, with discussions suggesting that cost-effective players like Chucky Hepburn could be a good fit for their roster.
